                        if d < best_d:
                            best_d = d
                            best_j = j
            tour.append(best_j)
            visited[best_j] = True
        return tour

    def two_opt(tour):
        pos = [0] * n
        for i, c in enumerate(tour):
            pos[c] = i
        improved = True
        dont_look = [False] * n
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for idx in range(n):
                if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
                    return tour
                if dont_look[tour[idx]]:
                    continue
                i = idx
                ci = tour[i]
                ci_next = tour[(i + 1) % n]
                d_ci = dist(ci, ci_next)
                found = False
                for cj in neighbors[ci]:
                    j = pos[cj]
                    if dist(ci, cj) >= d_ci:
                        break
                    j_next = (j + 1) % n
                    cj_next = tour[j_next]
                    if cj == ci or cj == ci_next:
                        continue
                    gain = d_ci + dist(cj, cj_next) - dist(ci, cj) - dist(ci_next, cj_next)
                    if gain > 0:
                        # Reverse segment between i+1 and j (or j+1 and i)
                        a, b = (i + 1) % n, j
                        if a <= b:
                            tour[a:b+1] = tour[a:b+1][::-1]
                        else:
                            # wrap-around
                            seg = tour[a:] + tour[:b+1]
                            seg.reverse()
                            tour[a:] = seg[:n-a]
                            tour[:b+1] = seg[n-a:]
                        for k2 in range(n):
                            pos[tour[k2]] = k2
                        improved = True
                        found = True
                        dont_look[ci] = False
                        dont_look[ci_next] = False
                        dont_look[cj] = False
                        dont_look[cj_next] = False
                        break
                if not found:
                    dont_look[ci] = True

    def or_opt(tour, seg_len):

        for i, c in enumerate(tour):
            pos[c] = i
        improved = True
        dont_look = [False] * n
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for idx in range(n):
                if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
                    return tour
                if dont_look[tour[idx]]:
                    continue
                i = idx
                # segment: tour[i], tour[i+1], ..., tour[i+seg_len-1]
                seg_start = i
                seg_end = (i + seg_len - 1) % n
                before_seg = (seg_start - 1) % n
                after_seg = (seg_end + 1) % n

                c_before = tour[before_seg]
                c_first = tour[seg_start]
                c_last = tour[seg_end]
                c_after = tour[after_seg]

                if c_before == c_after:
                    continue

                removal_gain = (dist(c_before, c_first) + dist(c_last, c_after)
                                - dist(c_before, c_after))

                found = False
                for cj in neighbors[c_first]:

                        return tour
                    j = pos[cj]
                    # try inserting segment after j
                    cj_next = tour[(j + 1) % n]

                    if in_seg:

                        dont_look[c_last] = False
                        dont_look[c_after] = False
                        dont_look[cj] = False
                        break
                if not found:
                    dont_look[tour[idx % len(tour)]] = True

    def local_search(tour):

        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
            return tour
        tour = or_opt(tour, 2)
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
            return tour
        tour = or_opt(tour, 3)
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
            return tour
        tour = two_opt(tour)
        return tour

    def double_bridge(tour):
        # Random double bridge perturbation (4-opt move)
        positions = sorted(random.sample(range(1, n), 4))
        a, b, c, d = positions
        new_tour = tour[:a] + tour[c:d] + tour[b:c] + tour[a:b] + tour[d:]
        return new_tour

    # Build initial tour with NN from best start
    best_tour = None
    best_len = float('inf')
    
    # Try a few NN starts
    starts = list(range(min(3, n)))
    for s in starts:
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.3:
            break
        t = nn_tour(s)
        l = tour_length(t)
        if l < best_len:
            best_len = l
            best_tour = t[:]

    # Local search on best NN tour
    current_tour = local_search(best_tour[:])
    current_len = tour_length(current_tour)
    if current_len < best_len:
        best_len = current_len
        best_tour = current_tour[:]

    # Iterated local search with double bridge perturbation
    while time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.92:
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.92:
            break
        perturbed = double_bridge(best_tour[:])
        perturbed = local_search(perturbed)
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
            p_len = tour_length(perturbed)
            if p_len < best_len:

                best_tour = perturbed[:]
            break
        p_len = tour_length(perturbed)
        if p_len < best_len:
            best_len = p_len
            best_tour = perturbed[:]

    return best_tour

import time
import math
import random

def solve(cities):
    t0 = time.time()
    n = len(cities)
    budget = 0.04 * n

    def dist(i, j):
        dx = cities[i][0] - cities[j][0]
        dy = cities[i][1] - cities[j][1]
        return int(math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) + 0.5)

    # Build distance cache for candidates
    K = min(10, n - 1)
    # nearest neighbors
    neighbors = []
    for i in range(n):
        dists = [(dist(i, j), j) for j in range(n) if j != i]
        dists.sort()
        neighbors.append([j for _, j in dists[:K]])

    # Greedy nearest-neighbor tour starting from best of a few starts
    def nn_tour(start):
        visited = [False] * n
        tour = [start]
        visited[start] = True
        cur = start
        for _ in range(n - 1):
            best_d = float('inf')
            best_j = -1
            for j in neighbors[cur]:
                if not visited[j]:
                    d = dist(cur, j)
                    if d < best_d:
                        best_d = d
                        best_j = j
            if best_j == -1:
                for j in range(n):
                    if not visited[j]:
                        d = dist(cur, j)
                        if d < best_d:
                            best_d = d
                            best_j = j
            tour.append(best_j)
            visited[best_j] = True
            cur = best_j
        return tour

    def tour_length(tour):
        return sum(dist(tour[i], tour[(i+1) % n]) for i in range(n))

    # Build initial tour
    best_tour = nn_tour(0)
    best_len = tour_length(best_tour)
    for s in range(1, min(n, 5)):
        if time.time() - t0 > budget * 0.2:
            break
        t = nn_tour(s)
        tl = tour_length(t)
        if tl < best_len:
            best_len = tl
            best_tour = t

    tour = best_tour[:]

    # pos array: pos[city] = index in tour
    pos = [0] * n
    for i, c in enumerate(tour):
        pos[c] = i

    def two_opt():
        improved = True
        dlb = [False] * n
        while improved:
            improved = False
            if time.time() - t0 > budget * 0.85:
                return
            for idx in range(n):
                if dlb[idx]:
                    continue
                if time.time() - t0 > budget * 0.85:
                    return
                i = idx
                ci = tour[i]
                ci_next = tour[(i + 1) % n]
                d1 = dist(ci, ci_next)
                found = False
                for cj in neighbors[ci]:
                    j = pos[cj]
                    if dist(ci, cj) >= d1:
                        break
                    j_next = (j + 1) % n
                    if j == i or j_next == i:
                        continue
                    cj_next = tour[j_next]
                    gain = d1 + dist(cj, cj_next) - dist(ci, cj) - dist(ci_next, cj_next)
                    if gain > 0:
                        # reverse segment between i+1 and j (or j+1 and i)
                        a, b = (i + 1) % n, j
                        # make sure a..b is the segment to reverse
                        # We need to reverse tour[a..b] (inclusive)
                        if a <= b:
                            seg = tour[a:b+1]
                            seg.reverse()
                            tour[a:b+1] = seg
                        else:
                            # wrap around - reverse a..n-1 + 0..b
                            seg = tour[a:] + tour[:b+1]
                            seg.reverse()
                            tour[a:] = seg[:n-a]
                            tour[:b+1] = seg[n-a:]
                        for k in range(n):
                            pos[tour[k]] = k
                        improved = True
                        found = True
                        dlb[ci] = False
                        dlb[ci_next] = False
                        dlb[cj] = False
                        dlb[cj_next] = False
                        break
                if not found:
                    dlb[i] = True

    def or_opt():
        # Or-opt: relocate segments of length 1, 2, 3
        improved = True
        dlb = [False] * n
        while improved:
            improved = False
            if time.time() - t0 > budget * 0.85:
                return
            for idx in range(n):
                if dlb[idx]:
                    continue
                if time.time() - t0 > budget * 0.85:
                    return
                found = False
                for seg_len in (1, 2, 3):
                    if found:
                        break
                    i = idx
                    # segment: tour[i], tour[i+1], ..., tour[i+seg_len-1]
                    i_prev = (i - 1) % n
                    i_end = (i + seg_len - 1) % n
                    i_next = (i + seg_len) % n

                    c_prev = tour[i_prev]
                    c_start = tour[i]
                    c_end = tour[i_end]
                    c_next = tour[i_next]

                    # cost of removing segment
                    remove_gain = dist(c_prev, c_start) + dist(c_end, c_next) - dist(c_prev, c_next)

                    for cj in neighbors[c_start]:
                        if time.time() - t0 > budget * 0.85:
                            return
                        j = pos[cj]
                        # skip if j is within the segment
                        # check if j is in [i, i_end]
                        in_seg = False
                        for k in range(seg_len):
                            if j == (i + k) % n:
                                in_seg = True
                                break
                        if in_seg or j == i_prev:
                            continue

                        cj_next = tour[(j + 1) % n]
                        if (j + 1) % n == i:
                            continue

                        # insert segment after j (forward)
                        insert_cost = dist(cj, c_start) + dist(c_end, cj_next) - dist(cj, cj_next)
                        gain = remove_gain - insert_cost
                        if gain > 0:
                            # rebuild tour
                            # extract segment
                            seg = [tour[(i + k) % n] for k in range(seg_len)]
                            # remove segment from tour
                            new_tour = []
                            k2 = i_next
                            while k2 != i:
                                new_tour.append(tour[k2])
                                k2 = (k2 + 1) % n
                            # find position of cj in new_tour
                            # insert seg after cj
                            ins_pos = new_tour.index(cj) + 1
                            new_tour = new_tour[:ins_pos] + seg + new_tour[ins_pos:]
                            if len(new_tour) == n:
                                tour[:] = new_tour
                                for k3 in range(n):
                                    pos[tour[k3]] = k3
                                improved = True
                                found = True
                                dlb[idx] = False
                                for s in seg:
                                    dlb[pos[s]] = False
                                break

                        # insert segment reversed after j
                        if seg_len > 1:
                            insert_cost_r = dist(cj, c_end) + dist(c_start, cj_next) - dist(cj, cj_next)
                            gain_r = remove_gain - insert_cost_r
                            if gain_r > 0:
                                seg = [tour[(i + k) % n] for k in range(seg_len)]
                                seg_r = seg[::-1]
                                new_tour = []
                                k2 = i_next
                                while k2 != i:
                                    new_tour.append(tour[k2])
                                    k2 = (k2 + 1) % n
                                ins_pos = new_tour.index(cj) + 1
                                new_tour = new_tour[:ins_pos] + seg_r + new_tour[ins_pos:]
                                if len(new_tour) == n:
                                    tour[:] = new_tour
                                    for k3 in range(n):
                                        pos[tour[k3]] = k3
                                    improved = True
                                    found = True
                                    dlb[idx] = False
                                    for s in seg_r:
                                        dlb[pos[s]] = False
                                    break
                if not found:
                    dlb[idx] = True

    # Run optimization passes
    two_opt()
    or_opt()

    current_len = tour_length(tour)
    if current_len < best_len:
        best_len = current_len
        best_tour = tour[:]

    # Perturbation + restart loop
    while time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.90:
        # double-bridge perturbation
        t2 = best_tour[:]
        # pick 4 random cut points
        positions = sorted(random.sample(range(n), 4))
        a, b, c, d = positions
        # double bridge: reconnect segments
        seg1 = t2[:a+1]
        seg2 = t2[a+1:b+1]
        seg3 = t2[b+1:c+1]
        seg4 = t2[c+1:]
        t2 = seg1 + seg3 + seg2 + seg4
        for i, c2 in enumerate(t2):
            pos[c2] = i
        tour[:] = t2

        two_opt()
        or_opt()

        current_len = tour_length(tour)
        if current_len < best_len:
            best_len = current_len
            best_tour = tour[:]

    return best_tour

 - Every active branch is ALREADY refined in place each round by its Experimenter (it iterates on its own best code). You don't need to ask for that — it happens automatically.
 - You may EVOLVE an existing branch: fork it (keeping its current best code) and push it in a specific new direction — the right move when a path is promising and you want a variation without losing its progress.
 - You may open brand-NEW hypotheses from scratch — for genuinely different directions the evidence now suggests.
(The Supervisor separately collapses weak branches and merges complementary pairs.) You are not limited to a fixed number of branches. Respond with a single JSON object only.

```python
import time
import math
import random

def solve(cities):
    t0 = time.time()
    n = len(cities)
    budget = 0.04 * n

    def dist(i, j):
        dx = cities[i][0] - cities[j][0]
        dy = cities[i][1] - cities[j][1]
        return int(math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) + 0.5)

    # Precompute candidate lists (k nearest neighbors)
    K = min(15, n - 1)
    candidates = []
    for i in range(n):
        dists = [(dist(i, j), j) for j in range(n) if j != i]
        dists.sort()
        candidates.append([j for _, j in dists[:K]])

    def tour_length(tour):
        return sum(dist(tour[i], tour[(i+1) % n]) for i in range(n))

    def nn_tour(start):
        visited = [False] * n
        tour = [start]
        visited[start] = True
        for _ in range(n - 1):
            cur = tour[-1]
            best_d = float('inf')
            best_j = -1
            for j in candidates[cur]:
                if not visited[j]:
                    d = dist(cur, j)
                    if d < best_d:
                        best_d = d
                        best_j = j
            if best_j == -1:
                for j in range(n):
                    if not visited[j]:
                        d = dist(cur, j)
                        if d < best_d:
                            best_d = d
                            best_j = j
            tour.append(best_j)
            visited[best_j] = True
        return tour

    def two_opt(tour):
        pos = [0] * n
        for i, c in enumerate(tour):
            pos[c] = i
        improved = True
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for idx in range(n):
                if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
                    return tour
                i = tour[idx]
                i_next = tour[(idx + 1) % n]
                d_i = dist(i, i_next)
                for j in candidates[i]:
                    j_idx = pos[j]
                    if j_idx == idx or j_idx == (idx + 1) % n:
                        continue
                    j_next = tour[(j_idx + 1) % n]
                    if dist(i, j) >= d_i:
                        break
                    gain = d_i + dist(i_next, j_next) - dist(i, j) - dist(i_next, j_next)
                    # Recompute properly
                    gain = d_i + dist(i_next, j_next) - dist(i, j) - dist(j_next, i_next)
                    gain = dist(i, i_next) + dist(j, j_next) - dist(i, j) - dist(i_next, j_next)
                    if gain > 0:
                        # Reverse segment between idx+1 and j_idx
                        a, b = (idx + 1) % n, j_idx
                        if a <= b:
                            tour[a:b+1] = tour[a:b+1][::-1]
                        else:
                            # wrap-around
                            seg = tour[a:] + tour[:b+1]
                            seg.reverse()
                            tour[a:] = seg[:n-a]
                            tour[:b+1] = seg[n-a:]
                        for k2, c in enumerate(tour):
                            pos[c] = k2
                        improved = True
                        break
        return tour

    def or_opt(tour):
        # Or-opt: relocate single cities and pairs
        pos = [0] * n
        for i, c in enumerate(tour):
            pos[c] = i
        improved = True
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for idx in range(n):
                if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
                    return tour
                # Try relocating city at idx
                prev_idx = (idx - 1) % n
                next_idx = (idx + 1) % n
                c = tour[idx]
                c_prev = tour[prev_idx]
                c_next = tour[next_idx]
                old_cost = dist(c_prev, c) + dist(c, c_next) - dist(c_prev, c_next)
                best_gain = 0
                best_ins = -1
                for nb in candidates[c]:
                    nb_idx = pos[nb]
                    if nb_idx == idx or nb_idx == prev_idx:
                        continue
                    nb_next = tour[(nb_idx + 1) % n]
                    if nb_next == c:
                        continue
                    gain = old_cost - dist(nb, c) - dist(c, nb_next) + dist(nb, nb_next)
                    if gain > best_gain:
                        best_gain = gain
                        best_ins = nb_idx
                if best_gain > 0:
                    city = tour.pop(idx)
                    ins = pos[tour[best_ins if best_ins < idx else best_ins - 1]] if False else best_ins
                    # Recompute insertion position after pop
                    ins_city = tour[best_ins if best_ins < idx else best_ins - 1] if best_ins != idx else -1
                    # Just redo with simpler logic
                    city = city  # already popped? No, let's redo
                    # Revert: put back
                    tour.insert(idx, city)
                    # Simpler: use list manipulation
                    c2 = tour[idx]
                    # Find insert position
                    ins_nb = -1
                    for nb in candidates[c2]:
                        nb_idx2 = pos[nb]
                        if nb_idx2 == idx or nb_idx2 == (idx-1)%n:
                            continue
                        nb_next2 = tour[(nb_idx2 + 1) % n]
                        if nb_next2 == c2:
                            continue
                        gain2 = old_cost - dist(nb, c2) - dist(c2, nb_next2) + dist(nb, nb_next2)
                        if gain2 > 0:
                            ins_nb = nb_idx2
                            break
                    if ins_nb >= 0:
                        tour.pop(idx)
                        ins2 = ins_nb if ins_nb < idx else ins_nb - 1
                        tour.insert(ins2 + 1, c2)
                        for k2, cc in enumerate(tour):
                            pos[cc] = k2
                        improved = True
        return tour

    def two_opt_simple(tour):
        improved = True
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for i in range(n - 1):
                if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.92:
                    return tour
                for j in range(i + 2, n):
                    if i == 0 and j == n - 1:
                        continue
                    a, b, c, d = tour[i], tour[i+1], tour[j], tour[(j+1) % n]
                    if dist(a, b) + dist(c, d) > dist(a, c) + dist(b, d):
                        tour[i+1:j+1] = tour[i+1:j+1][::-1]
                        improved = True
        return tour

    # Better 2-opt with position tracking
    def two_opt_v2(tour):
        improved = True
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for i in range(n):
                if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.92:
                    return tour
                ci = tour[i]
                ci1 = tour[(i+1) % n]
                d1 = dist(ci, ci1)
                for cj in candidates[ci]:
                    # Find j such that tour[j] == cj
                    pass
            break
        return tour

    best_tour = None
    best_len = float('inf')

    num_restarts = 0
    while time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.95:
        start = random.randint(0, n - 1)
        tour = nn_tour(start)
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.92:
            if best_tour is None:
                best_tour = tour
            break
        tour = two_opt_simple(tour)
        if time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.85:
            tour = or_opt(tour)
        tl = tour_length(tour)
        if tl < best_len:
            best_len = tl
            best_tour = tour[:]
        num_restarts += 1
        if num_restarts >= 3:
            break

    if best_tour is None:
        best_tour = list(range(n))

    return best_tour
```

```python
import time
import math
import random

def solve(cities):
    t0 = time.time()
    n = len(cities)
    budget = 0.04 * n

    xs = [c[0] for c in cities]
    ys = [c[1] for c in cities]

    def dist(i, j):
        dx = xs[i] - xs[j]
        dy = ys[i] - ys[j]
        return int(math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) + 0.5)

    # Precompute distance matrix for small n, else use function
    if n <= 300:
        D = [[0]*n for _ in range(n)]
        for i in range(n):
            for j in range(i+1, n):
                d = dist(i, j)
                D[i][j] = d
                D[j][i] = d
        def d(i, j): return D[i][j]
    else:
        def d(i, j):
            dx = xs[i] - xs[j]
            dy = ys[i] - ys[j]
            return int(math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) + 0.5)

    K = min(10, n - 1)
    candidates = []
    for i in range(n):
        dists = sorted((d(i, j), j) for j in range(n) if j != i)
        candidates.append([j for _, j in dists[:K]])

    def tour_length(tour):
        return sum(d(tour[i], tour[(i+1) % n]) for i in range(n))

    def nn_tour(start):
        visited = [False] * n
        tour = [start]
        visited[start] = True
        cur = start
        for _ in range(n - 1):
            best_d = 10**18
            best_j = -1
            for j in candidates[cur]:
                if not visited[j]:
                    dd = d(cur, j)
                    if dd < best_d:
                        best_d = dd
                        best_j = j
            if best_j == -1:
                for j in range(n):
                    if not visited[j]:
                        dd = d(cur, j)
                        if dd < best_d:
                            best_d = dd
                            best_j = j
            tour.append(best_j)
            visited[best_j] = True
            cur = best_j
        return tour

    def two_opt(tour):
        # position array
        pos = [0] * n
        for i in range(n): pos[tour[i]] = i
        dlb = [False] * n  # don't-look bits
        improved = True
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for idx in range(n):
                if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.9:
                    return tour
                ci = tour[idx]
                if dlb[ci]:
                    continue
                improved_i = False
                ci1 = tour[(idx + 1) % n]
                d1 = d(ci, ci1)
                for cj in candidates[ci]:
                    dij = d(ci, cj)
                    if dij >= d1:
                        break
                    j = pos[cj]
                    cj1 = tour[(j + 1) % n]
                    gain = d1 + d(cj, cj1) - dij - d(ci1, cj1)
                    if gain > 0:
                        # reverse segment between idx+1 and j (or j+1 and idx)
                        a = (idx + 1) % n
                        b = j
                        if a <= b:
                            tour[a:b+1] = tour[a:b+1][::-1]
                        else:
                            seg = tour[a:] + tour[:b+1]
                            seg.reverse()
                            tour[a:] = seg[:n - a]
                            tour[:b+1] = seg[n - a:]
                        for k2 in range(n): pos[tour[k2]] = k2
                        dlb[ci] = False
                        dlb[ci1] = False
                        dlb[cj] = False
                        dlb[cj1] = False
                        improved = True
                        improved_i = True
                        break
                if not improved_i:
                    dlb[ci] = True
        return tour

    def or_opt_pass(tour, seg_len):
        """Or-opt: relocate segments of length seg_len."""
        pos = [0] * n
        for i in range(n): pos[tour[i]] = i
        improved = False
        i = 0
        while i < n:
            if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.9:
                return tour, improved
            # segment: tour[i], tour[i+1], ..., tour[i+seg_len-1]
            idx = [((i + k) % n) for k in range(seg_len)]
            seg = [tour[x] for x in idx]
            prev_idx = (i - 1) % n
            next_idx = (i + seg_len) % n
            c_prev = tour[prev_idx]
            c_next = tour[next_idx]
            # cost of removing segment
            remove_gain = d(c_prev, seg[0]) + d(seg[-1], c_next) - d(c_prev, c_next)
            best_gain = 0
            best_j = -1
            best_rev = False
            for nb in candidates[seg[0]]:
                if nb in seg or nb == c_prev:
                    continue
                j = pos[nb]
                nb_next = tour[(j + 1) % n]
                if nb_next in seg:
                    continue
                gain = remove_gain - d(nb, seg[0]) - d(seg[-1], nb_next) + d(nb, nb_next)
                if gain > best_gain:
                    best_gain = gain
                    best_j = j
                    best_rev = False
                if seg_len > 1:
                    gain2 = remove_gain - d(nb, seg[-1]) - d(seg[0], nb_next) + d(nb, nb_next)
                    if gain2 > best_gain:
                        best_gain = gain2
                        best_j = j
                        best_rev = True
            if best_j >= 0:
                if best_rev:
                    seg = seg[::-1]
                # Remove segment from tour
                new_tour = [tour[k] for k in range(n) if k not in set(idx)]
                # Find insertion point
                ins_city = tour[best_j]
                ins_pos = new_tour.index(ins_city)
                for k2, s in enumerate(seg):
                    new_tour.insert(ins_pos + 1 + k2, s)
                tour = new_tour
                for k2 in range(n): pos[tour[k2]] = k2
                improved = True
            else:
                i += 1
        return tour, improved

    def full_or_opt(tour):
        for sl in [1, 2, 3]:
            if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.9:
                break
            changed = True
            while changed:
                if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.9:
                    break
                tour, changed = or_opt_pass(tour, sl)
        return tour

    def perturb(tour, strength=4):
        """Double-bridge perturbation."""
        t = tour[:]
        pos_list = sorted(random.sample(range(1, n), 4))
        a, b, c, d_ = pos_list
        new_tour = t[:a] + t[c:d_] + t[b:c] + t[a:b] + t[d_:]
        return new_tour

    best_tour = None
    best_len = float('inf')

    # Initial construction from multiple starts
    starts = list(range(min(5, n)))
    random.shuffle(starts)
    starts = starts[:3]

    for start in starts:
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.5:
            break
        tour = nn_tour(start)
        tour = two_opt(tour)
        if time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.8:
            tour = full_or_opt(tour)
        tl = tour_length(tour)
        if tl < best_len:
            best_len = tl
            best_tour = tour[:]

    if best_tour is None:
        best_tour = nn_tour(0)
        best_len = tour_length(best_tour)

    # ILS: iterated local search with double-bridge
    while time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.92:
        time_left = budget * 0.92 - (time.time() - t0)
        if time_left < 0.05:
            break
        tour = perturb(best_tour)
        tour = two_opt(tour)
        if time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.85:
            tour = full_or_opt(tour)
        tl = tour_length(tour)
        if tl < best_len:
            best_len = tl
            best_tour = tour[:]

    return best_tour
```

```python
import time
import math
import random

def solve(cities):
    t0 = time.time()
    n = len(cities)
    budget = 0.04 * n

    def dist(i, j):
        dx = cities[i][0] - cities[j][0]
        dy = cities[i][1] - cities[j][1]
        return int(math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) + 0.5)

    # Precompute k-nearest neighbors
    K = min(15, n - 1)
    neighbors = []
    for i in range(n):
        dists = [(dist(i, j), j) for j in range(n) if j != i]
        dists.sort()
        neighbors.append([j for _, j in dists[:K]])

    def tour_length(tour):
        return sum(dist(tour[i], tour[(i+1) % n]) for i in range(n))

    def nn_tour(start):
        visited = [False] * n
        tour = [start]
        visited[start] = True
        for _ in range(n - 1):
            cur = tour[-1]
            best_d = float('inf')
            best_j = -1
            for j in neighbors[cur]:
                if not visited[j]:
                    d = dist(cur, j)
                    if d < best_d:
                        best_d = d
                        best_j = j
            if best_j == -1:
                for j in range(n):
                    if not visited[j]:
                        d = dist(cur, j)
                        if d < best_d:
                            best_d = d
                            best_j = j
            tour.append(best_j)
            visited[best_j] = True
        return tour

    def two_opt(tour):
        pos = [0] * n
        for i, c in enumerate(tour):
            pos[c] = i
        improved = True
        dont_look = [False] * n
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for idx in range(n):
                if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
                    return tour
                if dont_look[tour[idx]]:
                    continue
                i = idx
                ci = tour[i]
                ci_next = tour[(i + 1) % n]
                d_ci = dist(ci, ci_next)
                found = False
                for cj in neighbors[ci]:
                    j = pos[cj]
                    if dist(ci, cj) >= d_ci:
                        break
                    j_next = (j + 1) % n
                    cj_next = tour[j_next]
                    if cj == ci or cj == ci_next:
                        continue
                    gain = d_ci + dist(cj, cj_next) - dist(ci, cj) - dist(ci_next, cj_next)
                    if gain > 0:
                        # Reverse segment between i+1 and j (or j+1 and i)
                        a, b = (i + 1) % n, j
                        if a <= b:
                            tour[a:b+1] = tour[a:b+1][::-1]
                        else:
                            # wrap-around
                            seg = tour[a:] + tour[:b+1]
                            seg.reverse()
                            tour[a:] = seg[:n-a]
                            tour[:b+1] = seg[n-a:]
                        for k2 in range(n):
                            pos[tour[k2]] = k2
                        improved = True
                        found = True
                        dont_look[ci] = False
                        dont_look[ci_next] = False
                        dont_look[cj] = False
                        dont_look[cj_next] = False
                        break
                if not found:
                    dont_look[ci] = True
        return tour

    def or_opt(tour, seg_len):
        pos = [0] * n
        for i, c in enumerate(tour):
            pos[c] = i
        improved = True
        dont_look = [False] * n
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for idx in range(n):
                if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
                    return tour
                if dont_look[tour[idx]]:
                    continue
                i = idx
                # segment: tour[i], tour[i+1], ..., tour[i+seg_len-1]
                seg_start = i
                seg_end = (i + seg_len - 1) % n
                before_seg = (seg_start - 1) % n
                after_seg = (seg_end + 1) % n

                c_before = tour[before_seg]
                c_first = tour[seg_start]
                c_last = tour[seg_end]
                c_after = tour[after_seg]

                if c_before == c_after:
                    continue

                removal_gain = (dist(c_before, c_first) + dist(c_last, c_after)
                                - dist(c_before, c_after))

                found = False
                for cj in neighbors[c_first]:
                    if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
                        return tour
                    j = pos[cj]
                    # try inserting segment after j
                    cj_next = tour[(j + 1) % n]
                    # make sure j is not in the segment
                    in_seg = False
                    for s in range(seg_len):
                        if j == (seg_start + s) % n or j == before_seg:
                            in_seg = True
                            break
                    if in_seg:
                        continue
                    insert_cost = (dist(cj, c_first) + dist(c_last, cj_next)
                                   - dist(cj, cj_next))
                    if removal_gain - insert_cost > 1e-10:
                        # Perform the move: extract segment and reinsert
                        # Build new tour
                        seg = []
                        for s in range(seg_len):
                            seg.append(tour[(seg_start + s) % n])
                        # Remove segment from tour
                        new_tour = []
                        idx2 = after_seg
                        for _ in range(n - seg_len):
                            new_tour.append(tour[idx2])
                            idx2 = (idx2 + 1) % n
                        # Find position of cj in new_tour
                        pos2 = {}
                        for k2, c2 in enumerate(new_tour):
                            pos2[c2] = k2
                        ins = pos2[cj]
                        new_tour = new_tour[:ins+1] + seg + new_tour[ins+1:]
                        tour = new_tour
                        for k2, c2 in enumerate(tour):
                            pos[c2] = k2
                        improved = True
                        found = True
                        dont_look[c_before] = False
                        dont_look[c_first] = False
                        dont_look[c_last] = False
                        dont_look[c_after] = False
                        dont_look[cj] = False
                        break
                if not found:
                    dont_look[tour[idx % len(tour)]] = True
        return tour

    def local_search(tour):
        tour = two_opt(tour)
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
            return tour
        tour = or_opt(tour, 1)
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
            return tour
        tour = or_opt(tour, 2)
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
            return tour
        tour = or_opt(tour, 3)
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
            return tour
        tour = two_opt(tour)
        return tour

    def double_bridge(tour):
        # Random double bridge perturbation (4-opt move)
        positions = sorted(random.sample(range(1, n), 4))
        a, b, c, d = positions
        new_tour = tour[:a] + tour[c:d] + tour[b:c] + tour[a:b] + tour[d:]
        return new_tour

    # Build initial tour with NN from best start
    best_tour = None
    best_len = float('inf')
    
    # Try a few NN starts
    starts = list(range(min(3, n)))
    for s in starts:
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.3:
            break
        t = nn_tour(s)
        l = tour_length(t)
        if l < best_len:
            best_len = l
            best_tour = t[:]

    # Local search on best NN tour
    current_tour = local_search(best_tour[:])
    current_len = tour_length(current_tour)
    if current_len < best_len:
        best_len = current_len
        best_tour = current_tour[:]

    # Iterated local search with double bridge perturbation
    while time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.92:
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.92:
            break
        perturbed = double_bridge(best_tour[:])
        perturbed = local_search(perturbed)
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
            p_len = tour_length(perturbed)
            if p_len < best_len:
                best_len = p_len
                best_tour = perturbed[:]
            break
        p_len = tour_length(perturbed)
        if p_len < best_len:
            best_len = p_len
            best_tour = perturbed[:]

    return best_tour
```

```python
import time

def solve(cities):

    n = len(cities)
```

```python
    xs = [c[0] for c in cities]
    ys = [c[1] for c in cities]

    def dist(i, j):

        dy = ys[i] - ys[j]

        for j in range(n):

                dx = xi - xs[j]
                dy = yi - ys[j]
                dists.append((dx*dx + dy*dy, j))
        dists.sort()
        neighbors.append([j for _, j in dists[:K]])

    def tour_length(tour):
        s = 0
        for i in range(n):
            s += dist(tour[i], tour[(i+1) % n])
        return s

    def nn_tour(start):
        visited = bytearray(n)
        tour = [start]
        visited[start] = 1
        for _ in range(n - 1):
            cur = tour[-1]
            best_d = 10**18
            best_j = -1
            for j in neighbors[cur]:
                if not visited[j]:
                    dx = xs[cur]-xs[j]; dy = ys[cur]-ys[j]
                    d = dx*dx+dy*dy
                    if d < best_d:
                        best_d = d
                        best_j = j
            if best_j == -1:
                for j in range(n):
                    if not visited[j]:
                        dx = xs[cur]-xs[j]; dy = ys[cur]-ys[j]
                        d = dx*dx+dy*dy
                        if d < best_d:
                            best_d = d
                            best_j = j
            tour.append(best_j)
            visited[best_j] = 1
        return tour

    def two_opt(tour):
        pos = [0] * n
        for i, c in enumerate(tour):
            pos[c] = i
        improved = True
        dont_look = bytearray(n)
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for idx in range(n):
                if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
                    return tour
                ci = tour[idx]
                if dont_look[ci]:
                    continue
                i = idx
                i1 = (i + 1) % n
                ci_next = tour[i1]
                d_ci = dist(ci, ci_next)
                found = False
                for cj in neighbors[ci]:
                    d_cj = dist(ci, cj)
                    if d_cj >= d_ci:
                        break
                    j = pos[cj]
                    j1 = (j + 1) % n
                    cj_next = tour[j1]
                    if cj == ci_next or cj_next == ci:
                        continue
                    gain = d_ci + dist(cj, cj_next) - d_cj - dist(ci_next, cj_next)
                    if gain > 0:
                        a, b = i1, j
                        if a <= b:
                            tour[a:b+1] = tour[a:b+1][::-1]
                        else:
                            seg = tour[a:] + tour[:b+1]
                            seg.reverse()
                            tour[a:] = seg[:n-a]
                            tour[:b+1] = seg[n-a:]
                        for k2 in range(n):
                            pos[tour[k2]] = k2
                        improved = True
                        found = True
                        dont_look[ci] = 0
                        dont_look[ci_next] = 0
                        dont_look[cj] = 0
                        dont_look[cj_next] = 0
                        break
                if not found:
                    dont_look[ci] = 1
        return tour

    def or_opt_pass(tour, seg_len):
        pos = [0] * n
        for i, c in enumerate(tour):
            pos[c] = i
        improved = True
        dont_look = bytearray(n)
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for idx in range(n):
                if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
                    return tour
                ci = tour[idx]
                if dont_look[ci]:
                    continue
                i = idx
                seg_end = (i + seg_len - 1) % n
                before_seg = (i - 1) % n
                after_seg = (seg_end + 1) % n

                c_before = tour[before_seg]
                c_first = tour[i]
                c_last = tour[seg_end]
                c_after = tour[after_seg]

                if c_before == c_after:
                    continue

                d_remove = dist(c_before, c_first) + dist(c_last, c_after)
                d_close = dist(c_before, c_after)
                removal_gain = d_remove - d_close

                best_gain = 1e-10
                best_j = -1
                best_rev = False

                for cj in neighbors[c_first]:
                    j = pos[cj]
                    in_seg = False
                    p = before_seg
                    for s in range(seg_len + 1):
                        if j == p:
                            in_seg = True
                            break
                        p = (p + 1) % n
                    if in_seg:
                        continue
                    cj_next = tour[(j + 1) % n]
                    insert_cost = dist(cj, c_first) + dist(c_last, cj_next) - dist(cj, cj_next)
                    g = removal_gain - insert_cost
                    if g > best_gain:
                        best_gain = g
                        best_j = j
                        best_rev = False
                    # Try reversed
                    insert_cost2 = dist(cj, c_last) + dist(c_first, cj_next) - dist(cj, cj_next)
                    g2 = removal_gain - insert_cost2
                    if g2 > best_gain:
                        best_gain = g2
                        best_j = j
                        best_rev = True

                if best_j != -1:
                    seg = []
                    for s in range(seg_len):
                        seg.append(tour[(i + s) % n])
                    if best_rev:
                        seg = seg[::-1]
                    new_tour = []
                    idx2 = after_seg
                    for _ in range(n - seg_len):
                        new_tour.append(tour[idx2])
                        idx2 = (idx2 + 1) % n
                    pos2 = {}
                    for k2, c2 in enumerate(new_tour):
                        pos2[c2] = k2
                    ins = pos2[tour[best_j % n]]
                    # find cj in new_tour
                    cj_city = tour[best_j]
                    ins = pos2[cj_city]
                    new_tour = new_tour[:ins+1] + seg + new_tour[ins+1:]
                    tour = new_tour
                    for k2, c2 in enumerate(tour):
                        pos[c2] = k2
                    improved = True
                    dont_look[c_before] = 0
                    dont_look[c_first] = 0
                    dont_look[c_last] = 0
                    dont_look[c_after] = 0
                    dont_look[cj_city] = 0
                else:
                    dont_look[ci] = 1
        return tour

    def local_search(tour):
        tour = two_opt(tour)
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
            return tour
        tour = or_opt_pass(tour, 1)
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
            return tour
        tour = or_opt_pass(tour, 2)
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
            return tour
        tour = or_opt_pass(tour, 3)
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
            return tour
        tour = two_opt(tour)
        return tour

    def double_bridge(tour):
        pos = sorted(random.sample(range(1, n), 4))
        a, b, c, d = pos
        return tour[:a] + tour[c:d] + tour[b:c] + tour[a:b] + tour[d:]

    # Build initial solutions
    best_tour = None
    best_len = float('inf')

    num_starts = max(1, min(5, n // 50))
    starts = random.sample(range(n), min(num_starts, n))
    for s in starts:
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.25:
            break
        t = nn_tour(s)
        l = tour_length(t)
        if l < best_len:
            best_len = l
            best_tour = t[:]

    current_tour = local_search(best_tour[:])
    current_len = tour_length(current_tour)
    if current_len < best_len:
        best_len = current_len
        best_tour = current_tour[:]

    # ILS
    no_improve = 0
    while time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.92:
        remaining = budget * 0.92 - (time.time() - t0)
        if remaining < 0.05:
            break
        perturbed = double_bridge(best_tour[:])
        perturbed = local_search(perturbed)
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.95:
            p_len = tour_length(perturbed)
            if p_len < best_len:
                best_len = p_len
                best_tour = perturbed[:]
            break
        p_len = tour_length(perturbed)
        if p_len < best_len:
            best_len = p_len
            best_tour = perturbed[:]
            no_improve = 0
        else:
            no_improve += 1

    return best_tour
```

```python
import time
import math
import random

def solve(cities):
    t0 = time.time()
    n = len(cities)
    budget = 0.04 * n

    def dist(i, j):
        dx = cities[i][0] - cities[j][0]
        dy = cities[i][1] - cities[j][1]
        return int(math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) + 0.5)

    # Build k-nearest neighbor lists
    K = min(20, n - 1)
    neighbors = []
    for i in range(n):
        dists = [(dist(i, j), j) for j in range(n) if j != i]
        dists.sort()
        neighbors.append([j for _, j in dists[:K]])

    def tour_length(tour):
        return sum(dist(tour[i], tour[(i+1) % n]) for i in range(n))

    # Nearest neighbor construction
    def nn_tour(start):
        visited = [False] * n
        tour = [start]
        visited[start] = True
        for _ in range(n - 1):
            cur = tour[-1]
            best_d = float('inf')
            best_j = -1
            for j in neighbors[cur]:
                if not visited[j]:
                    d = dist(cur, j)
                    if d < best_d:
                        best_d = d
                        best_j = j
                        break
            if best_j == -1:
                for j in range(n):
                    if not visited[j]:
                        d = dist(cur, j)
                        if d < best_d:
                            best_d = d
                            best_j = j
            tour.append(best_j)
            visited[best_j] = True
        return tour

    # Build position index
    def make_pos(tour):
        pos = [0] * n
        for i, c in enumerate(tour):
            pos[c] = i
        return pos

    # 2-opt with candidate lists
    def two_opt(tour):
        pos = make_pos(tour)
        improved = True
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for idx in range(n):
                if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.9:
                    return tour
                i = idx
                ci = tour[i]
                ci_next = tour[(i + 1) % n]
                d_ci = dist(ci, ci_next)
                for cj in neighbors[ci]:
                    j = pos[cj]
                    if j == i or j == (i + 1) % n:
                        continue
                    cj_next = tour[(j + 1) % n]
                    if cj_next == ci:
                        continue
                    gain = d_ci + dist(cj, cj_next) - dist(ci, cj) - dist(ci_next, cj_next)
                    if gain > 0:
                        # Reverse segment between i+1 and j
                        a, b = (i + 1) % n, j
                        if a <= b:
                            tour[a:b+1] = tour[a:b+1][::-1]
                        else:
                            # wrap around
                            seg = tour[a:] + tour[:b+1]
                            seg.reverse()
                            tour[a:] = seg[:n-a]
                            tour[:b+1] = seg[n-a:]
                        pos = make_pos(tour)
                        improved = True
                        break
        return tour

    # Or-opt: move single cities, pairs, triples
    def or_opt(tour, seg_len=1):
        pos = make_pos(tour)
        improved = True
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for idx in range(n):
                if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.9:
                    return tour
                # segment starts at idx
                i0 = idx
                i1 = (idx + seg_len - 1) % n
                prev_i = (i0 - 1) % n
                next_i1 = (i1 + 1) % n

                c_prev = tour[prev_i]
                c0 = tour[i0]
                c1 = tour[i1]
                c_next = tour[next_i1]

                if c_prev == c_next:
                    continue

                removal_gain = dist(c_prev, c0) + dist(c1, c_next) - dist(c_prev, c_next)

                # Try inserting segment after each neighbor of c0
                for cj in neighbors[c0]:
                    j = pos[cj]
                    # Don't insert in original position
                    if j == prev_i or j == i0 or (j >= i0 and j <= i1 and i0 <= i1) :
                        continue
                    cj_next = tour[(j + 1) % n]
                    if cj_next == c0:
                        continue
                    insert_cost = dist(cj, c0) + dist(c1, cj_next) - dist(cj, cj_next)
                    if removal_gain - insert_cost > 0:
                        # Perform the move
                        seg = [tour[(i0 + k) % n] for k in range(seg_len)]
                        # Remove segment
                        new_tour = []
                        k2 = next_i1
                        while k2 != i0:
                            new_tour.append(tour[k2])
                            k2 = (k2 + 1) % n
                        # Find insertion point
                        # Insert after cj in new_tour
                        ins_pos = None
                        for p, c in enumerate(new_tour):
                            if c == cj:
                                ins_pos = p
                                break
                        if ins_pos is None:
                            continue
                        new_tour = new_tour[:ins_pos+1] + seg + new_tour[ins_pos+1:]
                        if len(new_tour) == n:
                            tour = new_tour
                            pos = make_pos(tour)
                            improved = True
                            break
        return tour

    # Lin-Kernighan style 3-opt move (simplified or-opt with neighbor lists)
    def full_or_opt(tour):
        for seg_len in [1, 2, 3]:
            if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.85:
                break
            tour = or_opt(tour, seg_len)
        return tour

    # Run multiple starts
    best_tour = None
    best_len = float('inf')

    starts = list(range(min(n, 10)))
    random.seed(42)
    if n > 10:
        starts = random.sample(range(n), 10)

    for start in starts:
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.85:
            break
        tour = nn_tour(start)
        tour = two_opt(tour)
        if time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.85:
            tour = full_or_opt(tour)
        tl = tour_length(tour)
        if tl < best_len:
            best_len = tl
            best_tour = tour[:]

    # If time remains, do more 2-opt + or-opt on best
    while time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.9:
        if best_tour is None:
            break
        prev_len = best_len
        best_tour = two_opt(best_tour)
        if time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.85:
            best_tour = full_or_opt(best_tour)
        new_len = tour_length(best_tour)
        if new_len >= prev_len:
            break
        best_len = new_len

    if best_tour is None:
        best_tour = list(range(n))

    return best_tour
```

```python
import time
import math
import random

def solve(cities):
    t0 = time.time()
    n = len(cities)
    budget = 0.04 * n

    xs = [c[0] for c in cities]
    ys = [c[1] for c in cities]

    def dist(i, j):

        dy = ys[i] - ys[j]
        return int(math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) + 0.5)

    # Build distance matrix for small n, else use function
    if n <= 300:
        D = [[0]*n for _ in range(n)]
        for i in range(n):
            for j in range(i+1, n):
                d = dist(i, j)
                D[i][j] = d
                D[j][i] = d
        def d(i, j): return D[i][j]
    else:
        def d(i, j): return dist(i, j)

    # k-nearest neighbors
    K = min(15, n-1)
    neighbors = []
    for i in range(n):
        dists = [(dist(i, j), j) for j in range(n) if j != i]
        dists.sort()
        neighbors.append([x[1] for x in dists[:K]])

    def tour_length(tour):

            best_d = float('inf')
            best_j = -1
            for nb in neighbors[cur]:

                    dd = d(cur, nb)
                    if dd < best_d:
                        best_d = dd
                        best_j = nb
            if best_j == -1:
                for j in range(n):
                    if not visited[j]:
                        dd = d(cur, j)
                        if dd < best_d:
                            best_d = dd
                            best_j = j
            tour.append(best_j)
            visited[best_j] = True
        return tour

    def make_pos(tour):
        pos = [0]*n
        for i, c in enumerate(tour):
            pos[c] = i
        return pos

    def two_opt(tour, pos, cost):
        improved = True
        while improved:
            improved = False
            for i in range(n):
                if time.time() - t0 > budget * 0.85:
                    return tour, pos, cost
                ci = tour[i]
                ci1 = tour[(i+1)%n]
                d_ci_ci1 = d(ci, ci1)
                for nb in neighbors[ci]:
                    j = pos[nb]
                    cj = nb
                    cj1 = tour[(j+1)%n]
                    if cj1 == ci:
                        continue
                    gain = d_ci_ci1 + d(cj, cj1) - d(ci, cj) - d(ci1, cj1)
                    if gain > 0:

                        a, b = (i+1)%n, j
                        if a <= b:

                            seg.reverse()
                            tour[a:b+1] = seg
                        else:

                            seg = tour[a:] + tour[:b+1]
                            seg.reverse()
                            l1 = n - a
                            tour[a:] = seg[:l1]
                            tour[:b+1] = seg[l1:]
                        for k2, c2 in enumerate(tour):
                            pos[c2] = k2
                        cost -= gain
                        improved = True
                        break
        return tour, pos, cost

    def or_opt_move(tour, pos, cost, seg_len):

            improved = False
            for i in range(n):
                if time.time() - t0 > budget * 0.85:
                    return tour, pos, cost
                # segment: tour[i], tour[i+1], ..., tour[i+seg_len-1]
                idx = [(i + k) % n for k in range(seg_len)]
                seg = [tour[x] for x in idx]
                prev_i = tour[(i-1)%n]
                next_seg = tour[(i+seg_len)%n]

                # Cost of removing segment
                remove_gain = d(prev_i, seg[0]) + d(seg[-1], next_seg) - d(prev_i, next_seg)

                # Try inserting after each neighbor of seg[0]
                for nb in neighbors[seg[0]]:
                    if nb in set(seg):
                        continue
                    j = pos[nb]
                    jn = tour[(j+1)%n]
                    if jn == seg[0] or j == (i-1)%n:
                        continue
                    # Insert seg between j and jn
                    insert_cost = d(nb, seg[0]) + d(seg[-1], jn) - d(nb, jn)
                    gain = remove_gain - insert_cost
                    if gain > 0:
                        # Rebuild tour
                        new_tour = []
                        # Remove seg from tour
                        used = set(idx)
                        base = [tour[k] for k in range(n) if k not in used]
                        # Find position of nb in base
                        j2 = base.index(nb)
                        new_tour = base[:j2+1] + seg + base[j2+1:]
                        if len(new_tour) == n:
                            tour = new_tour
                            for k2, c2 in enumerate(tour):
                                pos[c2] = k2
                            cost -= gain
                            improved = True
                            break
                if improved:
                    break
        return tour, pos, cost

    # Start with NN tour from best of a few starts
    best_tour = None
    best_cost = float('inf')

    starts = [0]
    if n > 1:
        starts += random.sample(range(n), min(3, n))
    starts = list(set(starts))

    for s in starts:
        if time.time() - t0 > budget * 0.3:
            break
        t = nn_tour(s)
        c = tour_length(t)
        if c < best_cost:
            best_cost = c
            best_tour = t[:]

    tour = best_tour[:]
    pos = make_pos(tour)
    cost = best_cost

    # 2-opt
    tour, pos, cost = two_opt(tour, pos, cost)

    # Or-opt passes
    for sl in [1, 2, 3]:
        if time.time() - t0 > budget * 0.85:
            break
        tour, pos, cost = or_opt_move(tour, pos, cost, sl)

    # Another round of 2-opt
    if time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.85:
        tour, pos, cost = two_opt(tour, pos, cost)

    # More Or-opt
    for sl in [1, 2, 3]:
        if time.time() - t0 > budget * 0.9:
            break
        tour, pos, cost = or_opt_move(tour, pos, cost, sl)

    if cost < best_cost:
        best_cost = cost
        best_tour = tour[:]

    return best_tour
```

```python
import time
import math
import random

def solve(cities):
    t0 = time.time()
    n = len(cities)
    budget = 0.04 * n

    xs = [c[0] for c in cities]
    ys = [c[1] for c in cities]

    def dist(i, j):
        dx = xs[i] - xs[j]
        dy = ys[i] - ys[j]
        return int(math.sqrt(dx*dx + dy*dy) + 0.5)

    # k-nearest neighbors
    K = min(10, n-1)
    neighbors = []
    for i in range(n):
        dists = sorted((dist(i, j), j) for j in range(n) if j != i)
        neighbors.append([x[1] for x in dists[:K]])

    def tour_length(tour):
        return sum(dist(tour[i], tour[(i+1)%n]) for i in range(n))

    def nn_tour(start):
        visited = [False]*n
        tour = [start]
        visited[start] = True
        for _ in range(n-1):
            cur = tour[-1]
            best_d = float('inf')
            best_j = -1
            for nb in neighbors[cur]:
                if not visited[nb]:
                    dd = dist(cur, nb)
                    if dd < best_d:
                        best_d = dd
                        best_j = nb
            if best_j == -1:
                for j in range(n):
                    if not visited[j]:
                        dd = dist(cur, j)
                        if dd < best_d:
                            best_d = dd
                            best_j = j
            tour.append(best_j)
            visited[best_j] = True
        return tour

    def two_opt_pass(tour, pos, cost, dlb, time_limit):
        improved_any = False
        for idx in range(n):
            if time.time() - t0 > time_limit:
                return tour, pos, cost, dlb, improved_any
            i = idx
            if dlb[tour[i]]:
                continue
            ci = tour[i]
            ni = (i+1) % n
            ci1 = tour[ni]
            d_ci_ci1 = dist(ci, ci1)
            found = False
            for nb in neighbors[ci]:
                dval = dist(ci, nb)
                if dval >= d_ci_ci1:
                    break
                j = pos[nb]
                cj = nb
                cj1 = tour[(j+1)%n]
                if cj1 == ci or cj == ci1:
                    continue
                gain = d_ci_ci1 + dist(cj, cj1) - dval - dist(ci1, cj1)
                if gain > 0:
                    # reverse segment i+1..j
                    a, b = ni, j
                    if a <= b:
                        tour[a:b+1] = tour[a:b+1][::-1]
                    else:
                        seg = (tour[a:] + tour[:b+1])[::-1]
                        l1 = n - a
                        tour[a:] = seg[:l1]
                        tour[:b+1] = seg[l1:]
                    for k2 in range(n):
                        pos[tour[k2]] = k2
                    cost -= gain
                    dlb[ci] = False
                    dlb[ci1] = False
                    dlb[cj] = False
                    dlb[cj1] = False
                    found = True
                    improved_any = True
                    break
            if not found:
                dlb[tour[i]] = True
        return tour, pos, cost, dlb, improved_any

    def or_opt_pass(tour, pos, cost, dlb, seg_len, time_limit):
        improved_any = False
        for idx in range(n):
            if time.time() - t0 > time_limit:
                return tour, pos, cost, dlb, improved_any
            i = idx
            ci = tour[i]
            if dlb[ci]:
                continue
            seg_indices = [(i+k)%n for k in range(seg_len)]
            seg = [tour[x] for x in seg_indices]
            prev_i = tour[(i-1)%n]
            next_seg = tour[(i+seg_len)%n]

            if prev_i == next_seg:
                continue

            remove_gain = dist(prev_i, seg[0]) + dist(seg[-1], next_seg) - dist(prev_i, next_seg)

            found = False
            seg_set = set(seg)
            for nb in neighbors[seg[0]]:
                if nb in seg_set:
                    continue
                j = pos[nb]
                jn = (j+1) % n
                cjn = tour[jn]
                if cjn in seg_set:
                    continue
                if j == (i-1)%n or jn == seg_indices[0]:
                    continue
                insert_cost = dist(nb, seg[0]) + dist(seg[-1], cjn) - dist(nb, cjn)
                gain = remove_gain - insert_cost
                # also try reversed segment
                rev_insert = dist(nb, seg[-1]) + dist(seg[0], cjn) - dist(nb, cjn)
                rev_gain = remove_gain - rev_insert
                best_gain = max(gain, rev_gain)
                if best_gain > 0:
                    actual_seg = seg if gain >= rev_gain else seg[::-1]
                    # Rebuild tour
                    used = set(seg_indices)
                    base = [tour[k] for k in range(n) if k not in used]
                    j2 = base.index(nb)
                    new_tour = base[:j2+1] + actual_seg + base[j2+1:]
                    if len(new_tour) == n:
                        tour = new_tour
                        for k2 in range(n):
                            pos[tour[k2]] = k2
                        cost -= best_gain
                        for c2 in actual_seg + [prev_i, next_seg, nb, cjn]:
                            dlb[c2] = False
                        found = True
                        improved_any = True
                        break
            if not found:
                dlb[ci] = True
        return tour, pos, cost, dlb, improved_any

    def local_search(tour, cost, time_limit):
        pos = [0]*n
        for i in range(n):
            pos[tour[i]] = i
        dlb = [False]*n
        while time.time() - t0 < time_limit:
            tour, pos, cost, dlb, imp1 = two_opt_pass(tour, pos, cost, dlb, time_limit)
            if time.time() - t0 > time_limit:
                break
            imp2 = False
            for sl in [1, 2, 3]:
                if time.time() - t0 > time_limit:
                    break
                tour, pos, cost, dlb, imp = or_opt_pass(tour, pos, cost, dlb, sl, time_limit)
                imp2 = imp2 or imp
            if not imp1 and not imp2:
                break
        return tour, cost

    def double_bridge(tour):
        # 4-opt double bridge perturbation
        positions = sorted(random.sample(range(n), 4))
        a, b, c, d = positions
        new_tour = tour[:a+1] + tour[c+1:d+1] + tour[b+1:c+1] + tour[a+1:b+1] + tour[d+1:]
        return new_tour

    # Initial construction: best of multiple NN starts
    best_tour = None
    best_cost = float('inf')
    
    num_starts = max(1, min(n, int((budget * 0.15) / (n * 0.0001 + 0.01))))
    num_starts = min(num_starts, n)
    
    start_nodes = list(range(n))
    random.shuffle(start_nodes)
    start_nodes = start_nodes[:min(5, n)]
    
    for s in start_nodes:
        if time.time() - t0 > budget * 0.2:
            break
        t = nn_tour(s)
        c = tour_length(t)
        if c < best_cost:
            best_cost = c
            best_tour = t[:]

    # First local search pass
    time_for_first = t0 + budget * 0.5
    best_tour, best_cost = local_search(best_tour[:], best_cost, time_for_first)

    # ILS: iterated local search with double-bridge perturbation
    current_tour = best_tour[:]
    current_cost = best_cost

    while time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.95:
        remaining = budget * 0.95 - (time.time() - t0)
        if remaining < 0.05:
            break
        
        # Perturb
        new_tour = double_bridge(current_tour)
        new_cost = tour_length(new_tour)
        
        # Local search on perturbed tour
        time_limit_iter = min(t0 + budget * 0.95, time.time() + remaining * 0.5)
        new_tour, new_cost = local_search(new_tour, new_cost, time_limit_iter)
        
        if new_cost < best_cost:
            best_cost = new_cost
            best_tour = new_tour[:]
        
        # Accept if better (greedy acceptance)
        if new_cost <= current_cost:
            current_tour = new_tour[:]
            current_cost = new_cost
        else:
            # With small probability, accept worse
            current_tour = best_tour[:]
            current_cost = best_cost

    return best_tour
```

```python
        new_tour = tour[:a] + tour[c:d] + tour[b:c] + tour[a:b] + tour[d:]
        return new_tour

    best_tour = None
    best_len = float('inf')
    
    # Try a few NN starts

    for s in starts:
        if time.time() - t0 >= budget * 0.3:

        if l < best_len:
            best_len = l
            best_tour = t[:]

    # Local search on best NN tour
    current_tour = local_search(best_tour[:])
    current_len = tour_length(current_tour)
    if current_len < best_len:
        best_len = current_len
        best_tour = current_tour[:]

    # Iterated local search with double bridge perturbation
    while time.time() - t0 < budget * 0.92:

        perturbed = double_bridge(best_tour[:])

            p_len = tour_length(perturbed)

                best_tour = perturbed[:]
            break
        p_len = tour_length(perturbed)
        if p_len < best_len:
            best_len = p_len
            best_tour = perturbed[:]

    return best_tour
```
